title: Roku Q1 2023 Earnings Summary
text: Roku reported modest first-quarter results, with revenue climbing just 1% to $741 million, slightly exceeding Wall Street expectations of $708.49 million. The streaming platform operator posted a net loss of $193.6 million, or $1.38 per share. The company's streaming accounts reached 71.6 million by quarter's end, up 1.6 million from the previous year—surpassing analyst predictions. This growth underscored audience expansion despite challenging economic conditions. Streaming engagement hit record levels, with users consuming "3.9 streaming hours per active account per day" throughout the quarter. Total hours streamed across Roku's platforms jumped 20% year-over-year to 25.1 billion. Platform segment revenue declined 1% to $635 million. The company acknowledged that "The macro environment remained challenged in Q1," with advertising spend struggling in financial services and media sectors, though travel and wellness verticals improved. Operating costs surged 42% to $550 million, prompting Roku to implement two rounds of layoffs totaling 400 employees over six months. Management expressed caution about 2023, noting consumer pressures from inflation may continue dampening discretionary spending. Roku projected Q2 revenue around $770 million while targeting positive adjusted EBITDA by 2024.
